advice: Milk should usually be stored in the refrigerator at around 0-4°C. Higher temperatures increase bacterial growth and reduce shelf life.
Bread should be stored in a cool and dry place. High humidity can increase mold growth and reduce shelf life.
Meat is highly temperature-sensitive and should be stored at low temperature. If storage temperature is too high, spoilage risk increases quickly.
Fruits should be stored under suitable temperature and humidity conditions. Overripe fruits spoil faster, especially in warm environments.
Vegetables often need cool storage and moderate humidity. Too much moisture may cause decay, while too little moisture may cause drying.
Packaging condition affects shelf life. Opened or damaged packaging increases exposure to air and microorganisms, which can increase spoilage risk.
